definition: A charm is a spoken or written formula — a small verbal spell for healing, protection, or binding; to charm is to work such words upon someone, captivating or compelling them. It differs from enchantment in scale and register: a charm is the short, portable, everyday formula, often a scrap of prayer or verse, where enchantment implies a fuller, more theatrical working.
history: A charm is literally a song: Latin *carmen*, 'song, verse, incantation' — the same *carmen* as *Carmina Burana*. The turn is downward and outward: what began as sung verse became, in medieval practice, the humblest and commonest magic of all. As *The Magic of Rogues* observes, the most widespread form of magic in the middle ages was the use of charms, short formulae usually lifted from Scripture or liturgy for common ailments — mobile textual fragments that scholars can barely tell from prayer, medicine, or superstition. From there it is a short slide to the modern sense: a 'charming' person is, etymologically, still singing one over you. Magic scatters the word across dozens of cards.

def_sources.label: MedievalMagicDB, s.v. Charms (spoken/written formulae for healing, protection, coercion)
The Magic of Rogues (charms the most widespread medieval magic)

etymology.note: A charm is literally a song; a 'charming' person is faintly, etymologically, casting one. The same carmen as in Carmina Burana.
etymology.hook: Charm is a song that binds you.

cards.name: Edgar, Charmed Groom // Edgar Markov's Coffin
cards.type_line: Legendary Creature — Vampire Noble // Legendary Artifact

flavor_snippets.html:
"I don't care if it's an all-powerful relic or a street vendor's lucky <mark>charm</mark>. If it brings you courage, wear it."
—Saheeli Rai
In the harsh depths of Rootwater, the <mark>charms</mark> of surface dwellers are quite useless . . . except as lures.
Though they often bore little greater <mark>charm</mark> than the demons they battled, exorcists were always welcome in Scarwood.
"My grandmother had sold the rest of her jewelry long ago, but she left a particular necklace in my care: a golden chain, bearing a raven-shaped <mark>charm</mark>."
Barbarians weave its spurs into ceremonial <mark>charms</mark> to proclaim their hardiness in battle.
Noted neither for their good looks nor their <mark>charm</mark>, manticores can be fearsome allies. As dinner companions, however, they are best left alone.
Syr Branigan always remembered to kiss his good luck <mark>charm</mark>, having lost his foot to wolverines the last time he forgot.

"Cold iron's all you need to hold a faerie. The <mark>charms</mark> are mostly decorative."
—Eulyn, Edgewall peddler
The <mark>charm</mark> of a naga is undeniable.
<mark>Charm</mark> and grace may hide a foul heart.
Nomad and Nantuko, eagle and elephant; all the birds and beasts are <mark>charmed</mark> by his quiet dignity.
The druid knew only one spell, a <mark>charm</mark> to encourage rapid plant growth. When airborne Eternals invaded her arboretum, she had to get creative.
"You would never guess, at the terrifying sight of the man, that Hunding was as <mark>charming</mark> a companion as one could wish for."

mechanics_snippets.html: • Mardu <mark>Charm</mark> deals 4 damage to target creature.

Choose one —
• Naya <mark>Charm</mark> deals 3 damage to target creature.
• Return target card from a graveyard to its owner's hand.
• Tap all creatures target player controls.

Choose one —
• Boros <mark>Charm</mark> deals 4 damage to target player or planeswalker.
• Permanents you control gain indestructible until end of turn.
• Target creature gains double strike until end of turn.

Choose one —
• Put target creature on top of its owner's library.
• Jeskai <mark>Charm</mark> deals 4 damage to target opponent or planeswalker.
• Creatures you control get +1/+1 and gain lifelink until end of turn.
• Cabaretti <mark>Charm</mark> deals damage equal to the number of creatures you control to target creature or planeswalker.
• Maestros <mark>Charm</mark> deals 5 damage to target creature or planeswalker.

Lifelink
When this creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on each other creature you control named <mark>Charmed</mark> Stray.
• Cabaretti <mark>Charm</mark> deals damage equal to twice the number of creatures you control to target creature or planeswalker.
